title: Race-Lap Rank Calibration
summary: Pit decisions are competitive events within a race lap: only a small subset of drivers pit next lap, and strategy sources emphasize gaps, traffic, undercut/overcut, and pit delta rather than independent row probabilities.
rationale: Pit decisions are competitive events within a race lap: only a small subset of drivers pit next lap, and strategy sources emphasize gaps, traffic, undercut/overcut, and pit delta rather than independent row probabilities. ROC AUC rewards ordering, so rank-shaping within race context may help.
implementation_hint: After prediction, within each Race-Year-LapNumber group compute percentile rank of model score and blend final_score = 0.8*raw + 0.2*group_rank_score, optionally damped for groups with very few cars. Also add pre-model features for race-lap score priors: tyre-life percentile, pace-residual percentile, and position percentile.
expected_effect: Could improve global AUC by sharpening the ordering among drivers exposed to the same lap-level pit opportunity.
risk: May hurt if test rows are not complete race-lap grids or if probability calibration matters for leaderboard despite AUC being rank-based.
